raw_consumer_statement:
What happened : On XX/XX/year>, I was charged {$2400.00} by XXXX  XXXX XXXX XXXX for what was supposed to be high-quality, thick hair extensions professionally installed by the owner. Instead, I received poor-quality, thin, stringy extensions that did not match my natural hair XXXX and were installed incorrectly using fusion extensions. 
The incorrect installation has caused hair breakage and balding. Another professional stylist examined my hair and expressed concern about the damage caused. 
The merchant attempted XXXX correction, but the results were still unacceptable. When I requested a proper fix, the owner refused to do the work herself ( despite charging me owner pricing ) and insisted a different stylist would have to do it. She then demanded that I sign a contract promising not to complain or dispute the charge before she would fix anything further. I refused. 
My banks mishandling : I filed a dispute with Barclays. They denied my claim on XX/XX/year>. The denial appears to have overlooked : Photo evidence showing poor-quality and mismatched extensions Proof of improper installation Evidence of physical harm ( hair loss ) Documentation of the merchants coercive behavior A XXXX  stylists evaluation Attempts to resolve the issue directly with the merchant I believe Barclays did not consider all the evidence or fully evaluate the quality-of-service issues and physical damage. 
What I want : I am requesting that the CFPB require Barclays to : XXXX. Reopen and reinvestigate the dispute XXXX Review all evidence XXXX. Provide a fair outcome according to the Fair Credit Billing Act XXXX. Refund the {$2400.00} for the damaged and improperly performed service Why this matters : I paid for professional-quality services and instead received defective work that harmed my hair and required additional corrective care. The merchant also attempted to silence complaints through a forced contract. This situation has caused financial loss, stress, and physical damage.
